definition_en: The subjects studied in a school or educational institution
example: The school updated its curriculum to include coding.

definition_en: The ability to read and write
example: Improving literacy rates is a key goal in developing countries.

definition_en: Teaching, especially in small groups or privately; also the fee for education
example: She paid her university tuition with a scholarship.

definition_en: A grant of money given to a student to help pay for education
example: He won a scholarship to study abroad.

definition_en: A long piece of written research submitted for a university degree
example: She spent a year writing her dissertation on climate policy.

definition_en: The process of judging a student's knowledge or performance
example: Continuous assessment replaces final exams in some schools.

definition_en: Copying someone else's work and presenting it as your own
example: Plagiarism can result in expulsion from university.

definition_en: To officially register to attend a course or institution
example: She enrolled in an online English course.

definition_en: A student who is studying for their first university degree
example: He is an undergraduate at the University of Buenos Aires.

definition_en: A student studying after their first degree, such as a Master's or PhD
example: She is doing postgraduate research in linguistics.

definition_en: The list of topics covered in a course or subject
example: The syllabus for the exam is available on the website.

definition_en: Required by law or rules; not optional
example: Education is compulsory for children between 6 and 16.

definition_en: A person who leaves school or university without finishing
example: The school aims to reduce the number of dropouts.

definition_en: Activities done outside the regular school timetable
example: She was involved in many extracurricular activities.

definition_en: A small class at university for discussion and study of a topic
example: The seminar on globalisation was very thought-provoking.

definition_en: A talk given to a large group of students at university
example: The professor delivered an interesting lecture on economics.

definition_en: Work that a student does at home
example: Don't forget to do your homework before the next class.

definition_en: A person who teaches a student individually
example: She hired a tutor to help her with maths.

definition_en: Able to speak two languages fluently
example: Their children grew up bilingual in Spanish and English.

definition_en: The ability to analyse information and make reasoned judgements
example: Schools should develop students' critical thinking skills.

definition_en: The evaluation of work by people of the same level or expertise
example: Her article was accepted after peer review.

definition_en: A method of studying where students and teachers are not in the same place
example: Distance learning became essential during the pandemic.
